summary.summary: Removed explicit Core Data fetchLimit parameters from multiple data-fetch calls so those fetches rely on time-based NSPredicate predicates instead of a hard record count. The diffs show the fetchLimit values removed in: APSManager (glucose ranges/getGlucose calls), PumpHistoryStorage (pumpHistoryLast24h), BolusCalculationManager (glucose fetch), LiveActivity DataManager (six-hour fetch), and AppleWatchManager (glucose fetch). The change is committed as 8fc5e8626997 and merged via edfea8c6c198; original PR: pr:nightscout/Trio#1254. (See diff:b0c3b5b5e2a8 and the listed changed files for exact edits.)
summary.userFacingImpact: According to the PR description and diffs, this removes hard limits that could truncate recent pump-history and glucose fetch results (e.g., getPumpHistory previously used a fetchLimit of 288 which could truncate >24h of events when many events exist) and therefore may prevent missing recent pump events or glucose entries that fall within the time predicates (pr:gordolio/Trio#1254; pr:nightscout/Trio#1254; diff:b0c3b5b5e2a8). The change may also increase the number of fetched records per query compared to before; any performance or memory impact is not described in the supplied sources and should be validated by a human reviewer (file:ab5cfe3bb235; file:2b9506403c06; file:f0243669aa90; file:9bdb95b6f674; file:d07c6ddbb57f).
